text.title: workflow-spec-phase Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for workflow-spec-phase as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Copying constitution sections from a different project or from training data without validating against the actual codebase.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Constitution copied without validation becomes "aspirational" rather than descriptive. When executor agents follow the constitution but the codebase contradicts it, they produce inconsistent code that fails review.

failure-mode.description: Over-engineering the constitution for a new project by specifying microservices, message queues, and multi-region deployment from day one.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Premature architectural complexity locks in decisions that should emerge from actual usage. Start with the simplest architecture that could work; the constitution can be updated when complexity is justified.

failure-mode.description: Agent makes assumptions instead of asking questions when user says "I don't know".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "I don't know" is valid input that must be documented as uncertainty, not interpolated. Agents that fill uncertainty with plausible answers produce specs that look complete but contain fabricated requirements.

failure-mode.description: Writing large blocks of spec without incremental validation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Errors in section 1 propagate to sections 2-8. Show and validate each section before proceeding; fixing a requirement after five dependent sections are written requires rewriting all downstream sections.
